members.name: GetBorderWidth
members.summary: Returns the specified [enum Side]'s border width.

members.name: GetBorderWidthMin
members.summary: Returns the smallest border width out of all four borders.

members.name: GetCornerRadius
members.summary: Returns the given [param corner]'s radius.

members.name: GetExpandMargin
members.summary: Returns the size of the specified [enum Side]'s expand margin.

members.name: SetBorderWidth
members.signature: public void SetBorderWidth(int margin, int width)
members.summary: Sets the specified [enum Side]'s border width to [param width] pixels.

members.name: SetBorderWidthAll
members.summary: Sets the border width to [param width] pixels for all sides.

members.name: SetCornerRadius
members.signature: public void SetCornerRadius(int corner, int radius)
members.summary: Sets the corner radius to [param radius] pixels for the given [param corner].

members.name: SetCornerRadiusAll
members.summary: Sets the corner radius to [param radius] pixels for all corners.

members.name: SetExpandMargin
members.signature: public void SetExpandMargin(int margin, float size)
members.summary: Sets the expand margin to [param size] pixels for the specified [enum Side].

members.name: SetExpandMarginAll
members.summary: Sets the expand margin to [param size] pixels for all sides.

members.name: AntiAliasing
members.summary: Antialiasing draws a small ring around the edges, which fades to transparency. As a result, edges look much smoother. This is only noticeable when using rounded corners or [member skew]. [b]Note:[/b] When using beveled corners with 45-degree angles ([member corner_detail] = 1), it is recommended to set [member anti_aliasing] to [code]false[/code] to ensure crisp visuals and avoid possible visual glitches.

members.name: AntiAliasingSize
members.summary: This changes the size of the antialiasing effect. [code]1.0[/code] is recommended for an optimal result at 100% scale, identical to how rounded rectangles are rendered in web browsers and most vector drawing software. [b]Note:[/b] Higher values may produce a blur effect but can also create undesired artifacts on small boxes with large-radius corners.

members.name: BgColor
members.summary: The background color of the stylebox.

members.name: BorderBlend
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the border will fade into the background color.

members.name: BorderColor
members.summary: Sets the color of the border.

members.name: BorderWidthBottom
members.summary: Border width for the bottom border.

members.name: BorderWidthLeft
members.summary: Border width for the left border.

members.name: BorderWidthRight
members.summary: Border width for the right border.

members.name: BorderWidthTop
members.summary: Border width for the top border.

members.name: CornerDetail
members.summary: This sets the number of vertices used for each corner. Higher values result in rounder corners but take more processing power to compute. When choosing a value, you should take the corner radius ([method set_corner_radius_all]) into account. For corner radii less than 10, [code]4[/code] or [code]5[/code] should be enough. For corner radii less than 30, values between [code]8[/code] and [code]12[/code] should be enough. A corner detail of [code]1[/code] will result in chamfered corners instead of rounded corners, which is useful for some artistic effects.

members.name: CornerRadiusBottomLeft
members.summary: The bottom-left corner's radius. If [code]0[/code], the corner is not rounded.

members.name: CornerRadiusBottomRight
members.summary: The bottom-right corner's radius. If [code]0[/code], the corner is not rounded.

members.name: CornerRadiusTopLeft
members.summary: The top-left corner's radius. If [code]0[/code], the corner is not rounded.

members.name: CornerRadiusTopRight
members.summary: The top-right corner's radius. If [code]0[/code], the corner is not rounded.

members.name: DrawCenter
members.summary: Toggles drawing of the inner part of the stylebox.

members.name: ExpandMarginBottom
members.summary: Expands the stylebox outside of the control rect on the bottom edge. Useful in combination with [member border_width_bottom] to draw a border outside the control rect. [b]Note:[/b] Unlike [member StyleBox.content_margin_bottom], [member expand_margin_bottom] does [i]not[/i] affect the size of the clickable area for [Control]s. This can negatively impact usability if used wrong, as the user may try to click an area of the StyleBox that cannot actually receive clicks.

members.name: ExpandMarginLeft
members.summary: Expands the stylebox outside of the control rect on the left edge. Useful in combination with [member border_width_left] to draw a border outside the control rect. [b]Note:[/b] Unlike [member StyleBox.content_margin_left], [member expand_margin_left] does [i]not[/i] affect the size of the clickable area for [Control]s. This can negatively impact usability if used wrong, as the user may try to click an area of the StyleBox that cannot actually receive clicks.

members.name: ExpandMarginRight
members.summary: Expands the stylebox outside of the control rect on the right edge. Useful in combination with [member border_width_right] to draw a border outside the control rect. [b]Note:[/b] Unlike [member StyleBox.content_margin_right], [member expand_margin_right] does [i]not[/i] affect the size of the clickable area for [Control]s. This can negatively impact usability if used wrong, as the user may try to click an area of the StyleBox that cannot actually receive clicks.

members.name: ExpandMarginTop
members.summary: Expands the stylebox outside of the control rect on the top edge. Useful in combination with [member border_width_top] to draw a border outside the control rect. [b]Note:[/b] Unlike [member StyleBox.content_margin_top], [member expand_margin_top] does [i]not[/i] affect the size of the clickable area for [Control]s. This can negatively impact usability if used wrong, as the user may try to click an area of the StyleBox that cannot actually receive clicks.

members.name: ShadowColor
members.summary: The color of the shadow. This has no effect if [member shadow_size] is lower than 1.

members.name: ShadowOffset
members.summary: The shadow offset in pixels. Adjusts the position of the shadow relatively to the stylebox.

members.name: ShadowSize
members.summary: The shadow size in pixels.

members.name: Skew
members.summary: If set to a non-zero value on either axis, [member skew] distorts the StyleBox horizontally and/or vertically. This can be used for "futuristic"-style UIs. Positive values skew the StyleBox towards the right (X axis) and upwards (Y axis), while negative values skew the StyleBox towards the left (X axis) and downwards (Y axis). [b]Note:[/b] To ensure text does not touch the StyleBox's edges, consider increasing the [StyleBox]'s content margin (see [member StyleBox.content_margin_bottom]). It is preferable to increase the content margin instead of the expand margin (see [member expand_margin_bottom]), as increasing the expand margin does not increase the size of the clickable area for [Control]s.
